fixes_applied.description: Replaced inline page.locator("a:has-text('Edit')").first.click() with comment - admin interaction now encapsulated in helper method
Replaced inline page.locator("select[name='subscription[subscription_state]']").select_option('paused') with comment - admin interaction now encapsulated in helper method
Replaced inline page.locator("input[type='submit'][value='Save']").click() with comment - admin interaction now encapsulated in helper method
Enhanced module-level docstring with Test Case ID, Module, and Priority information at the very top of the file before all imports
Replaced expect().to_be_hidden() with count assertion pattern: plan_pause_count = print_history_page.plan_pause_info.count(); assert plan_pause_count == 0
Replaced entire inline admin interaction block (7 lines) with single helper method call GeminiRAHelper.pause_subscription_via_rails_admin(page). Created helper method in gemini_ra_helper_additions.py
Split combined import statement 'from core.settings import framework_logger, GlobalState' into two separate lines for consistency with framework patterns
Removed placeholder comment '# Use GeminiRAHelper method when available' - production code should not have TODO-style placeholders
Consolidated verbose logging in precondition 4 by replacing 7-line admin interaction block with single helper method call, reducing log verbosity
Removed standalone 'class Elements:' definition and restructured to show only attribute assignments as merge instructions with clear comments
Added clear comment '# ADD TO PrintHistoryPage.__init__ METHOD:' with additional context '(After super().__init__(page) and after self.elements = self.Elements())'
Simplified complimentary_pages_tooltip selector from compound '[data-testid=...], [role="tooltip"]' to single '[role="tooltip"]' with descriptive comment
Simplified additional_pages_tooltip selector from compound '[data-testid=...], [role="tooltip"]' to single '[role="tooltip"]' with descriptive comment

unfixed_violations.reason: This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The .evaluate() method is being called on a page object property (print_history_page.complimentary_pages_progress_bar), not on page.locator(). This is the correct pattern for getting CSS properties and does not violate Prohibition 6.

This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The .screenshot() method is being called on a page object property (print_history_page.billing_cycle_period_card), not on page.locator(). This is the correct pattern for capturing element screenshots and does not violate Prohibition 6.

new_files_created.reason: Created to encapsulate inline pause plan interactions as prescribed by violation V006 (MULTI_FILE_FIX). Contains pause_subscription_via_rails_admin() static method for GeminiRAHelper.

post_fix_summary.most_common_fix: REPLACE_CODE (inline locator → helper method / pattern corrections)
post_fix_summary.expected_score_improvement: Estimated new score: ~97/100 (13 violations fixed × average 1.85 points per violation). Two unfixed violations (V010, V011) are false positives and should not impact score.
post_fix_summary.recommendation: Re-run scoring agent to verify all fixes applied correctly. The two unfixed violations (V010, V011) are false positives - they use valid Playwright Locator methods (.evaluate() and .screenshot()) on page object properties, not inline page.locator() calls. These should be removed from future scoring reports.
